evals.prompt: Créer un export PDF pour liste des paiements avec header logo, tableau, badges statut colorés
key_differences: with_skill: DejaVu Sans font (correct for French accents) vs without_skill: Arial
with_skill: table-based header layout vs without_skill: div-based header (unreliable in DomPDF)
with_skill: .row-even td/.row-odd td selectors (correct DomPDF fix) vs without_skill: :nth-child (NOT supported in DomPDF)
with_skill: stat cards section with 4 KPIs vs without_skill: no stat cards
with_skill: tfoot with total row vs without_skill: no total row

evals.prompt: Fixer les background-color sur <tr> qui ne s'affichent pas dans DomPDF
key_differences: Both correctly identify the tr background bug
without_skill adds $loop->even Blade trick for :nth-child replacement (bonus insight)
with_skill concise and focused; without_skill slightly more comprehensive on :nth-child fix
Non-discriminating eval — both baseline and skill get 100%

evals.prompt: Créer exportToPDF() dans ESBTPAttendanceController avec header base64, stats, couleurs KLASSCI
key_differences: with_skill: isPhpEnabled=false included (security); without_skill: missing this option
with_skill: Method signature uses type-hinted ESBTPEtudiant model injection vs without_skill: fetches from auth()->user()->etudiant
with_skill: Comments reference skill sections (§4, §8, §12) for future maintainability
with_skill: 3 fallback paths for logo; without_skill: 2 fallback paths

analyst_observations: Eval 1 is the most discriminating: with_skill wins 8/8 vs 6/8 for without_skill. The critical failures in without_skill are: (1) Arial font instead of DejaVu Sans — will cause garbled French accents in production PDFs; (2) :nth-child CSS used for row striping — this is a DomPDF limitation that without_skill was unaware of, meaning alternating rows will NOT render.

Eval 2 is non-discriminating: both achieve 100%. The tr→td background fix is well-documented online. Consider replacing with a harder test (e.g., multi-column layout, @font-face usage, or pixel-perfect preview matching).

Eval 3 is nearly non-discriminating at the formal assertion level, but qualitative differences exist: with_skill adds isPhpEnabled=false (security hardening) and uses Route Model Binding (better Laravel pattern). Consider adding assertions for isPhpEnabled.
Speed: with_skill tends to be faster on evals 1 and 2 (148s vs 161s, 107s vs 170s) but slower on eval 3 (226s vs 208s). Token usage is roughly comparable.

Recommendation: The skill delivers clear value on eval 1 (the realistic PDF creation task). The DomPDF-specific knowledge (DejaVu Sans, :nth-child limitation, table layouts) is genuinely useful and not obvious from general knowledge.
